title: cli.py: extract-text command
description: Wire `pypptx extract-text <file.pptx> [--slides 1,3,5] [--output file.txt]` into `cli.py`. Parse the `--slides` comma-separated list into a `list[int]`. Without `--output`, write plain text directly to stdout (bypassing `output_result()`). With `--output`, write text to the file and output JSON `{"output_file": "...", "slide_count": N}` to stdout.
acceptance_criteria: `pypptx extract-text deck.pptx` writes plain text with `--- Slide N ---` delimiters to stdout regardless of `--plain`.
`pypptx extract-text deck.pptx --slides 1,3` outputs only slides 1 and 3.
`pypptx extract-text deck.pptx --output out.txt` writes text to `out.txt` and outputs `{"output_file": "out.txt", "slide_count": N}` to stdout.
`--plain` with `--output` outputs just the filename to stdout.
Invalid slide indices (out of range, non-integer) go to stderr; exit code is non-zero.
Errors go to stderr; exit code is non-zero on failure.
